phrases.intent_en: How do I pay for parking
phrases.cultural_note: Parking payment machines in Czech shopping centers typically accept both cards and coins.

likely_response.local: V automatu před výtahem, karta nebo mince.
likely_response.translation_en: At the machine before the elevator, card or coins.

responses.english: At the machine before the elevator, card or coins.
responses.french: V automatu před výtahem, karta nebo mince.

phrases.intent_en: Where do I buy gift cards
phrases.cultural_note: Gift cards ('dárkové poukázky') are usually sold at a dedicated desk near the main entrance.

likely_response.translation_en: At the counter to the right of the entrance.

responses.english: At the counter to the right of the entrance.

phrases.intent_en: Is the customer service desk open now
phrases.cultural_note: Customer service desks in Czech malls often close at 19:00 or 20:00.

likely_response.translation_en: Yes, until seven in the evening.

responses.english: Yes, until seven in the evening.

phrases.cultural_note: Staff often use compass-like directions (rovně=straight) combined with floor levels.

likely_response.local: Rovně a pak doleva do přízemí.
likely_response.translation_en: Straight ahead and then left on the ground floor.

responses.english: Straight ahead and then left on the ground floor.
responses.french: Rovně a pak doleva do přízemí.

phrases.intent_en: Where is the ATM in the main lobby
phrases.cultural_note: Czech ATMs are commonly called 'bankomat' and are often placed near escalators or entrances.

likely_response.translation_en: In the corner next to the escalators.

responses.english: In the corner next to the escalators.
